6016.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Germans on 6 May 1944, not repaired and broken up for scrap.

6017.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Germans on 26 December 1943, not repaired and broken up for scrap.

6018.history: Blown up at Casablanca, Morocco by one of her own torpedoes.

6019.history: Disarmed at Alexandria, Egypt and interned by the British on 22 June 1940. Rejoined the Allied cause and rearmed on 30 May 1943. Stricken 10 December 1952.

6020.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6021.history: Sunk by Gunfire from Allied warships off Casablanca, Morocco during the Allied landing in North Africa.

6022.history: Sunk by gunfire from Allied warships off Casablanca,Morocco during the Allied landing in North Africa.(Capitaine De Corvette Charles Marie Pierre Jean Martinant De Preneuf the commanding officer was amongst those killed.

6023.history: Sunk by gunfire from Allied warships off Casablanca, Morocco during the Allied landing in North Africa.

6024.history: Sunk by gunfire from Allied warships off Casablanca, Morocco during the Allied landing in North Africa.

6025.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6025.history: Disarmed at Alexandria, Egypt and interned by the British on 22 June 1940. Rejoined the Allied cause and rearmed on 30 May 1943. Stricken 10 November 1952.

6026.history: Foudroyant (Capitaine De Fregate Paul Louis Antoine Fontaine) was bombed and sunk by German aircraft off Dunkirk. The wreck lies in 28 meters of water in position 51º04'58"N, 02º15'23"E.
commanders: Paul Louis Antoine Fontaine, FR ( - 1 Jun 1940)

6027.history: Bison (Capitaine De Vaisseau Jean Adolphe Roger Bouan) was sunk by German aircraft some 110 nautical miles west of Vega Island, Norway in postion 65º42'N, 07º17'E. The Commanding officer was killed in the sinking
commanders: Jean Adolphe Roger Bouan, FR ( - 3 May 1940 (+))

6028.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in 1943, but not repaired. Sunk by Allied aircraft on 11 March 1944.

6029.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians and commissioned in the Italian Navy as FR 21. Scuttled by the Italians at La Spezia on 9 September 1943.

6030.built_by: At.&Ch de St. Nazaire (St. Nazaire, France)
6030.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in 1943 and renamed FR 24. Scuttled at La Spezia on 9 September 1943. Raised by the Germans and sunk in Genoa in 1945.

6031.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6031.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised in September 1943 but not repaired.

6032.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6033.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Italians on 10 July 1943. Sunk at Toulon by Allied aircraft on 24 November 1943.

6034.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6034.history: Operated by the Vichy French and based at Casablanca during the Allied landing in North Africa in November 1942. Damaged on 8 November 1942 by American aircraft. Repaired and rejoined the Allied cause. Used as gunnery training ship until stricken on 9 September 1959.

6035.history: Ran aground off Oran, Algeria after being damaged by gunfire from the British light cruiser HMS Aurora.

6036.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Italians on 7 June 1943, but not repaired. Sunk by Allied aircraft on 7 March 1944.

6037.history: Ran aground at Casablanca, Morocco after being damaged by American naval gunfire and American aircraft.

6038.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6038.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in July 1943 but not repaired. Sunk by Allied aircraft in February 1944.

6039.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6040.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6040.history: Sunk off Syria in position 35º20'N, 35º20'E by British torpedo bombers.

6041.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6041.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6042.built_by: At.&Ch de St. Nazaire (St. Nazaire, France)
6042.history: Maille Breze (Capitaine De Fregate Henry Marie Edouard Antoine Glotin) was severely damaged by a two of its own torpedoes at Greenock, Scotland. She was scuttled by her crew to prevent her magazines from exploding and causing major damage to the civilian areas nearby. Two live torpedoes were accidentally discharged into her own forward section causing a terrific explosion with flames shooting 50 feet high into the air. Some of her crewmembers tried to squeeze themelves through the portholes to safety. There were 25 dead and 48 wounded among her crew.
commanders: Henry Marie Edouard Antoine Glotin, FR ( - 30 Apr 1940)

6043.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6043.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6044.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6045.history: Beached and shot on fire on 23 September 1940 by gunfire from the Australian heavy cruiser HMAS Australia during a British attack on Dakar. Refloated and towed to Bizerte, Tunisia but sunk there by Allied aircraft on 7 May 1943. Refloated again on 14 December 1943 but not worth repairing.

6046.history: Operated by the Vichy French and based at Dakar during the Allied landing in North Africa in November 1942. Rejoined the Allied cause. Stricken 2 May 1957.

6047.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6047.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6048.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6048.history: Operated by the Vichy French and based at Dakar during the Allied landing in North Africa in November 1942. Rejoined the Allied cause. Decomissioned on 1 February 1964. Used as a breakwater in 1965.

6049.history: Operated by the Vichy French and based at Dakar during the Allied landing in North Africa in November 1942. Rejoined the Allied cause. Stricken 1 June 1962 and sold for scrap.

6050.history: Captured by the British at Plymouth on 3 July 1940. Joined the Free French Navy on 28 august 1940. Decommissioned on 6 December 1954 and scrapped in 1958.

6051.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Italians on 5 April 1943 but not repaired.

6052.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated 20 May 1943 but not repaired.

6053.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6053.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in 1943, towed to Italy for repairs and renamed FR 37. Captured at Savona, Italy by the Germans on 9 September 1943. Scuttled by the Germans in April 1944.

6054.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6054.history: Renamed Foudroyant in 1941. Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in 1943 and renamed FR 36. Captured by the Germans at Toulon on 9 September 1943. Scuttled by the Germans at Toulon in August 1944.

6055.history: Renamed L'Adroit in 1941. Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised in 1943 by the Italians and renamed FR 33. Captured by the Germans at Toulon on 9 September 1944. Captured by the Allies in 1944.

6056.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6056.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6057.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6057.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6058.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised in 1943 by the Italians, towed to Italy and renamed FR 34. Captured by the Germans on 9 September 1943 at Imperia. Scuttled by the Germans in 1945.

6059.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6059.history: Renamed Sirocco in 1941. Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in 1943 and renamed FR 32. Captured by the Germans at Toulon on 9 September 1943. Scuttled by the Germans in August 1944.

6060.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6060.history: Renamed Bison in 1941. Never completed. Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in 1943 and renamed FR 35. Captured by the Germans at Toulon on 9 September 1943. Scuttled at Toulon by the Germans in August 1944.

6061.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French Navy on 31 August 1940. Laid up in reserve at Hartepool U.K. in 1943. Returned to Cherbourg on 16 September 1945. Stricken 15 May 1950.

6062.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French Navy but laid up in reserve at Hartelpool, U.K. Returned to Cherbourg on 9 September 1945. Stricken 31 August 1950.

6063.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6063.history: Captured by the Italians at Bizerte, Tunisia on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 42. Transferred to Germany on 6 April 1943 and renamed TA 10. Heavily damaged by gunfire from the British destroyer HMS Eclipse on 23 September 1943. Scuttled at Rhodos on 27 September 1943.

6064.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6064.history: Captured by the Italians at Bizerte, Tunisia on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 43. Transferred to Germany on 6 April 1943 and renamed TA 11. Sunk on 10 September 1943 at Piombino by Italian motor torpedo boats and gunfire from Italian tanks.

6065.built_by: Ch Maritime du Sud Ost (Bordeaux, France)
6065.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians on 28 April 1943, repaired and renamed FR 44. Captured by the Germans and renamed TA 13. Scuttled by the Germans at Toulon on 25 August 1944.

6066.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French Navy but laid up in reserve at Greenock, Scotland. Returned to Cherbourgh on 23 September 1945. Stricken 17 February 1950.

6067.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Returned to the Free French Navy but laid up in reserve at Hartepool, U.K. Returned to Cherbourg on 9 September 1945. Stricken 31 August 1950.

6068.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in July 1943 but not repaired.

6069.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6069.history: Captured by the Italians at Bizerte, Tunisia on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 41. Transferred to Germany on 6 April 1943 and renamed TA 9. Sunk at Toulon on 23 August 1944 by Allied aircraft.

6070.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Lost 14 December 1940 during a storm 25 miles south-south-west off Eddystone while she was serving in the Royal Navy.

6071.history: Captured by the British while at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Royal Netherlands Navy on 31 August 1940. Returned by the Dutch to the Royal Navy on 12 January 1941 and immediately transferred to the Free French navy. Used as Asdic school ship until August 1943. Laid up in reserve at Hartepool, U.K. in 1944. Stricken 31 August 1950.

6072.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians on 13 May 1943 and renamed FR 45. Captured by the Germans on 9 September 1943 and renamed TA 12. Sunk by American aircraft on 24 November 1943.

6073.history: Captured by the Italians at Bizerte, Tunisia on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 113. Captured by the Germans on 9 September 1943. Further fate unknown.

6074.history: Souffleur (Lieutanant De Vaisseau Benoit Lejay) was torpedoed and sunk on 25 June 1941 off Beyrut, Lebanon in position 33º49'N, 35º26'E by the British submarine HMS Parthian.

6075.history: Escaped from Toulon on 27 November 1942 and rejoined the Allies. Disarmed at Oran in April 1944. Stricken 28 February 1946.

6076.history: Disarmed at Bizerte, Tunisia in April 1941.. Captured there by the Italians on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 115. Captured by the Germans on 9 September 1943. Scuttled on 15 September 1943.

6077.history: Joined the Free French Navy at Malta at the time of the French surrender. Mined off Tunisia on or around 15 December 1940.

6078.history: Morse (Lieutenant De Vaisseau. Jean Georges Charles Paris) was mined and sunk off Kerkenna on 16 June 1940.
commanders: Jean Georges Charles Paris, FR ( - 16 Jun 1940 (+))

6079.history: Disarmed at Bizerte, Tunisia in April 1941. Captured there by the Italians on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 111. FR 111 was sunk on 28 February 1943 10 miles off Murro di Porco, Sicily by Allied aircraft.

6080.history: Seized on 3 July 1940 by the British while at Plymouth. Transferred to the Free Fench Navy. Surcouf (Capitaine De Fregate Georges Louis Nicolas Blaison) was sunk on 18 February 1942 in the Gulf of Mexico in position 10º40'N, 79º31'W after a collision with the American merchant ship Thomson Lykes. There were no survivors, 130 officers and men (including some British) went down with the ship.

6081.history: Disarmed at Bizerte, Tunisia in April 1941. Captured there by the Italians on 8 December 1942 and renamed FR 114. Scuttled by the Italians at the Castellamare shipyard on 13 September 1943. Raised by the Germans in 1943 but not repaired.

6082.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised in February 1943. Sunk 11 March 1944 by Allied aircraft.

6083.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6083.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in March 1943 but not repaired. Bombed and sunk at Toulon by allied aircraft on 22 June 1944.

6084.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6084.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Italians on 17 March 1943 but sunk again on 17 April 1943. Raised again on 17 July 1943. Sunk on 24 November 1943 during an Allied air raid.

6085.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6085.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians. Bombed and sunk at Toulon by allied aircraft on 22 June 1944.

6086.history: Disarmed at Oran in October 1940 and scuttled there on 9 November 1942.

6087.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians. Bombed and sunk at Toulon by allied aircraft on 22 June 1944.

6088.history: Disarmed at Oran in October 1940. Scuttled at Oran on 9 November 1942.

6089.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6089.history: Disarmed at Bizerte in December 1940 and captured there on 8 December 1942 by the Italians. Renamed FR 117 by the Italians. Scuttled at Bizerte on 6 May 1943. Raised by the Allies but not repaired. Finally stricken on 18 August 1947.

6090.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6090.history: Disarmed at Bizerte in December 1940 and captured there by the Italians on 8 December 1942. Sunk at Bizerte during an Allied air raid on 31 January 1943.

6091.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6091.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians on 1 March 1943 but not repaired.

6092.history: At 00.14 hours on 9 May 1940, the Doris (Capitaine De Corvette Jean Ernest Marie Favreul) was torpedoed and sunk about 30 nautical miles west of Petten, Netherlands in position 52º47'N, 03º49'E by the German submarine U-9. See also this article about the discovery of the Doris. Hit by U-boatSunk on 9 May 1940 by U-9 (Lüth).

6093.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Italians in 1943. Sunk 11 March 1944 by Allied aircraft.

6094.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6095.history: Based in North Africa during the Allied landings. Rejoined the Allied cause. Stricken 19 February 1952.

6096.built_by: At.&Ch de St. Nazaire-Penhoet (St. Nazaire, France)
6096.history: Based at Oran during the Allied landings in North Africa but escaped to Toulon. Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated on 29 January 1943. Sunk again on 19 February 1943. Refloated again on 4 May 1943. Sunk by Allied aircraft on 11 March 1944.

6097.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians in June 1943 and renamed FR 118. Captured in Genoa by the Germans on 9 September 1943. Further fate unknown.

6098.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6098.history: Monge (Capitaine de corvette (Lt.Cdr.) Marcel Auguste Jean Delort), was sunk off Diego Saurez on 8 May 1942 by the British destroyers HMS Active and HMS Panther.

6099.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians on 5 June 1943. Sunk by Allied aircraft on 11 March 1944.

6100.history: Scuttled at Brest on 18 June 1940 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6101.history: Sunk off Gabon in position 00º20'S, 08º50'E by the British sloop HMS Milford.

6102.history: Scuttled at Brest on 18 June 1940 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Achille was in drydock and unable to move under own power when the germans entered the city, leaving no alternative but to scuttle.

6103.history: Sunk off Dakar on 24 September 1940 by the British destroyer HMS Fortune. Ajax had left harbor in an attempt to attack the Anglo-FNFL forces offshore, but was detected on sonar, depth charges, forced to the surface and finally sunk by gunfire shortly after 0700 hrs.

6104.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6104.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Italians 26 June 1943. Sunk by Allied aircraft on 24 November 1943.

6105.history: Stationed in North Africa during the Allied landings. Rejoined the Allied cause. Stricken on 26 April 1946.

6106.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6106.history: Disarmed at Alexandria, Egypt and interned by the British on 22 June 1940. Rejoined the Allied cause and rearmed on 30 May 1943. Departed Algiers for patrol off Southern France on 18 December 1943. Did not return from patrol. The wreck was found on 6 April 1995 in position 43°04'16"N, 5°32'14"E. Evidence seems to indicate that she was mined.

6107.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6107.history: Arrived at Saigon, Indo-China on 6 March 1941 and disarmed there. Stricken 10 June 1950.

6108.history: Sunk off Dakar on 23 September 1940 by the British destroyers HMS Foresight and HMS Inglefield.

6109.history: Disarmed at Bizerte, Tunisia and captured there by the Italians on 8 December 1942. Renamed FR 112 by the Italians. Captured by the Germans at Napels, Italy on 15 September 1943. Further fate unknown.

6110.history: Disarmed at Bizerte, Tunisia in June 1940 and captured there by the Italians on 8 December 1942. Scuttled by the Italians off the Tunisian coast on 6 May 1943. Raised by the Allies but not repaired. Finally stricken on 12 August 1947.

6111.history: Disarmed at Bizerte, Tunisia and captured there on 8 December 1942 by the Italians. Sunk at Bizerte during an Allied air raid on 31 January 1943. Raised but not repaired. Finally stricken on 12 August 1947.

6112.history: Seized by the British on 3 July 1940 at Dundee, Scotland. Transferred to the Free French. Stricken 4 October 1949.
commanders: Cdr. Robert Michael Gore Gambier, RN (3 Jul 1940 - 11 Jul 1940)

6113.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans. Refloated by the Italians on 29 March 1943. Bombed and sunk at Toulon by allied aircraft on 22 June 1944.

6114.history: Joined the Allies after the Allied landings in North Africa. Sunk in error by British aircraft in the North Atlantic in position 55º27'N, 33º50'W while returning to Britain from the USA after refitting there.

6115.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6115.history: Sunk off Oran on 8 November 1942 during the Allied landings in North Africa by the British destroyers HMS Achates and HMS Westcott.

6116.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6116.history: Rejoined the Allies after the landing in North Africa. Stricken 23 March 1946.

6117.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6117.history: Rejoined the Allies after the landings in North Africa. Stricken on 23 March 1946.

6118.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6118.history: Rejoined the Allies after the landings in North Africa. Stricken 14 August 1946.

6119.built_by: Chantiers Schneider et Cie (Chalon sur Saône, France)
6119.history: Rejoined the Allies after the landings in North Africa. Stricken 27 December 1946.

6121.history: Damaged off Casablanca on 8 November 1942 by American aircraft. Beached at Mazagan north off Cape Blanco in position 33º25'N, 08º40'W on 10 November 1942 and wrecked after being bombed again by an aircraft from the American light cruiser USS Philadelphia (CL 41).

6122.history: Escaped from Casablanca to Dakar during the Allied landing in North Africa and rejoined the Allies. Stricken 26 April 1946.

6123.history: On 8 November 1942 Amphitrite was at Casablanca when Operation Torch (the Allied landings in North Africa) began. Before she had a chance of getting ready for sea, she was sunk at her moorings in position 33º06'30"N, 07º36'58"W by gunfire from the support group (US battleship Massachusetts, cruisers and destroyers) and air attacks from USN planes flying from the carrier CV.4 Ranger. Raised by the Allies. Stricken in May 1946.

6124.history: Escaped from Casablanca to Dakar during the Allied landings in North Africa and rejoined the Allies. Stricken 26 April 1946.

6125.history: Rejoined the Allies after the Allied landings in North Africa after being captured at Casablanca. Stricken 15 April 1946.
commanders: Capitaine De Corvette Louis Eugene Yves Marie Courson, FR (3 Sep 1939 - 10 Jun 1940)
Lieutenant De Vaisseau Robert Henri Auguste Meynier, FR (10 Jun 1940 - 15 Mar 1942)

6126.history: Sunk in Casablanca harbour in position 33º06'30"N, 07º36'58"W on 8 November 1942 by American aircraft. Raised by the Allies in 1944 but not repaired.

6127.history: Left Casablanca harbour on 8 November and is reported missing thereafter, it is possible that she was bombed and sunk by a US aircraft, but there is a report of a submarine that sank in an Allied defensive minefield near Casablanca during the night of 16/17 November. Some sources claim that this submarine was sunk by U-173, but this is very unlikely.

6128.history: Sunk in Casablanca harbour in position 33º06'30"N, 07º36'58"W on 8 november 1942 by American aircraft during the Allied landings in North Africa. Raised in 1944 but not repaired.(Lieutenant De Vaisseau Maurice Marie Victor Stanislas Guittet) who was killed in the sinking.

6129.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6129.history: Seized by the British on 3 July 1940 while in Portsmouth. Stricken in April 1943 and cannibalized for spare parts.

6130.history: Seized by the British on 3 July 1940 while in Portsmouth. Stricken in April 1943 and cannibalized for spare parts.

6131.history: Scuttled at Toulon on 27 November 1942 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6132.built_by: At.&Ch de St. Nazaire-Penhoet (St. Nazaire, France)
6132.history: Escaped on 27 November 1942 from Toulon to Oran to rejoin the Allies. Stricken 27 October 1952.

6133.history: Rejoined the Allies after the Allied landings in North Africa. Stricken 19 June 1952.

6134.history: Sunk off Diego Suarez, Madagascar in position 12º03'45"S, 49º03'30"E by aircraft from the British aircraft carrier HMS Illustrious.

6135.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6135.history: Sunk 13 November 1942 off Dakhla, Morocco in position 24º22'N, 15º47'W by two American Catalina's from VP-92. The commanding officer Capitaine De Corvette Jean Eugene Leon Lefevre was killed

6136.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
6136.history: Scuttled off Cadiz, Spain by her own crew on 15 November 1942.(Lieutenant De Vaisseau Maurice Eugene Leon Paumier)The Commanding officer was killed ashore?

6137.history: Scuttled at Brest on 18 June 1940 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6138.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6138.history: Sunk off Cape Juby, Morocco by U-37 in approximate position 28º03'N, 12º54'W. Her commander Lieutenant De Vaisseau Marcel Julien Marie Groix and 64 men were killed in the sinking, there were only 4 survivors. Hit by U-boatSunk on 19 Dec 1940 by U-37 (Clausen).

6139.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
6139.history: Escaped from Toulon on 27 November 1942 and joined the Allies at Algiers. Stricken on 12 February 1952.

6140.history: On 5 May 1942 she was riding at anchor in Currier Bay, Diego Suarez, when the British launched their invasion of Madagascar (Operation "Ironclad"). The French ships in the anchorage were attacked without warning by FAA planes from the British aircraft carriers HMS Indomitable and HMS Illustrious, which secured bomb hits on the sub, sinking her in position 12º16'30"S, 49º17'5"E. Raised by the Allies in April 1943 but survey declared her unfit for repairs and subsequently scrapped. Stricken on 26 December 1946.

6141.history: Scuttled at Brest on 18 June 1940 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

6142.history: Sunk off Fedhala roads, Morocco in position 33º35'N, 07º50'W by an aircraft from the American escort carrier USS Suwannee (CVE 27).

6143.history: Seized by the British in Plymouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French in September 1940. Carried pennant number P 26 during the Second World War. On 19 September 1945 Minerve ran aground after her tow line parted. She lies of Chesil Beach, Portland Bill (position 50°31'01N 02°27'06W) in 10 meters of water. She is reported to be badly broken up.
